answer: The September 15, 2026 Florida escrow reconciliation overstatement for FNF National Title closings was caused by the settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 pipeline incorrectly joining party_identity_bridge using normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id. The normalized_tax_id key matched multiple buyer and seller party roles within the same closing, duplicating escrow disbursements in the finance.disbursement_recon_daily table. Remediation requires switching to the correct join key, quarantining the old join pattern, and backfilling affected records.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Switch the transform to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.root_cause: settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joining party_identity_bridge by normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id, which matched multiple buyer and seller party roles inside the same closing, duplicating escrow disbursements
